text: Meri partner ki shaadi thi par dadi ki seva ke chakkar mein main ja nahi payi aur ab ghar wale mere liye rishte dekh rahe hain. Main unhe apni sacchai bata kar unki 'izzat' mitti mein nahi mila sakti, isliye chup-chap unki pasand ke ladke se milne ko taiyaar hoon. Parivaar ki shaan bani rahe, bas isiliye main apni asli pehchaan ko andar hi maar rahi hoon taaki 'log kya kahenge' wala darr unhe na sataye.

Ghar ki dekhbhaal aur restricted mobility ki wajah se main uski shaadi mein nahi ja saki, aur ab Mummy ko lagta hai ki main bas ek 'sanskari beti' hoon jo ghar sambhal rahi hai. Agar maine apni LGBTQ identity reveal ki toh Papa samaj mein mooh dikhane layak nahi rahenge, isliye main unki har baat maanti hoon. Unka sar uncha rahe, bas isi liye main apne pyaar aur haq ki baatein kabhi zubaan par nahi laati.

Caregiving mein itni thak gayi thi ki apni sabse achi dost ki shaadi miss ho gayi, par asli dard ye hai ki main kabhi apni partner ko 'bahu' banakar ghar nahi la paungi. Main ghar mein koi kalesh nahi chahti isliye shaadi ke pressure par hamesha muskura kar haan bol deti hoon. Khandaan ki maryada bachane ke liye maine apni feelings ko ek kone mein dafan kar diya hai taaki meri parvarish par koi sawal na uthe.

Dadi ki tabiyat kharab thi toh mera bahar nikalna band ho gaya, aur main us wedding ka hissa nahi ban saki jahan mujhe thodi sukoon milti. Main apni identity chhupaye rakhti hoon kyunki hamare samaj mein ye sab 'paap' mana jata hai aur main nahi chahti ki meri wajah se ghar walon ko taane sunne padein. Unki khushi ke liye main har din ek natak jee rahi hoon taaki rishton mein koi doori na aaye.

Shaadi mein na ja paane ka dukh toh hai, par usse bada dukh ye hai ki main kabhi apne asli roop mein maa-baap ke samne nahi aa sakti. Agar maine sacchai batayi toh ghar ki 'izzat' chali jayegi, isliye main chup-chap wahi karti hoon jo ek ideal beti ko karna chahiye. Main parivaar ka maan bachane ke liye apni poori zindagi ek jhoot mein bita dungi, kyunki mere liye unka samman meri khushi se zyada zaroori hai.
